model_inputs.note: Elo diff 464.87 is LARGEST in our analysis series. Larger than FRA-IRQ (392.5), ARG-AUT (304.5), POR-UZB (237.6)

rules_applied.RULE7: FIRES — Ghana 3.97% win prob < 15% threshold. Submit unshaded estimates for all Ghana offensive props. Do not compress toward 50%.
rules_applied.RULE8: Does NOT fire — draw 9.48% < 25% threshold. No general attacking shrinkage.
rules_applied.RULE15: FIRES — Semenyo as expected-losing-team forward in massive mismatch. Suppress Q9.
rules_applied.RULE1: Match winner: avg(sharp market, model). Both converge at ~86%.
rules_applied.FLOOR_0.25: Applied to Q10 (Ghana corners), Q9 partially. All count-stat questions floored at 0.25.
rules_applied.RULE_FOULS: Q2: predict which team DEFENDS. Ghana is the clear defending team from kickoff. Ghana fouls more.

england_vs_croatia_4_2.england_tactical_style: High press, high line, 4-2-3-1. Tuchel counter-pressing. Defensive vulnerabilities exposed (Croatia scored 2).
england_vs_croatia_4_2.key_note: England scored at 12' and 42' in 1H — active first-half scorers.

ghana_vs_panama_1_0.semenyo_note: Man of the Match despite 0 SOT. ESPN final box confirms 0 SOT.
ghana_vs_panama_1_0.ghana_1h_note: CRITICAL: Ghana had ZERO total shots in the first half vs Panama. Not just 0 SOT — 0 total shots.
ghana_vs_panama_1_0.ghana_tactical_style: 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 deep block. Invited possession (62% Panama). Dangerous on transitions. Classic counter-attacking setup.
ghana_vs_panama_1_0.partey_status: ABSENT GD1 (visa issue). Thomas Partey returns for GD2 — key defensive midfielder.
ghana_vs_panama_1_0.goalkeeper_note: Lawrence Ati-Zigi injured at HT vs Panama. Benjamin Asare (backup) expected to start GD2.

referee_data.name: Said (Héctor Saíd) Martínez Sorto

wc2026_gd1.note: Penalty awarded in first WC match. 3 yellows.

referee_data.career_penalty_rate: not confirmed — only WC2026: 1 in 1 match
referee_data.assessment: High card-giver (career 4.17 YC/game vs WC avg ~2-3). Awarded 1 penalty in only WC match. Ghana fouling to defend = elevated card/penalty probability.

harry_kane.fitness: No concerns — fully expected to start
harry_kane.context: One goal vs Ghana = sole England WC record (surpassing Gary Lineker 10 goals)

antoine_semenyo.club_2024_25: Bournemouth (PL) then Manchester City from January 2026
antoine_semenyo.club_sot_per_90_note: Career estimate only — specific 2024-25 per-90 not confirmed. Man City stint 17 goals in 37 matches.
antoine_semenyo.gd1_note: CRITICAL: ESPN final box shows 0 SOT vs Panama despite being Man of the Match. All 2 shots were blocked/off-target.
antoine_semenyo.market: No FanDuel/DraftKings lines found — MAIN_BOOK_ABSENCE signal for scorer markets

group_l_standings.context: Both teams 3 pts. England lead on GD. A win clinches strong position for England. Ghana need points to stay level with England at top. GD3: England vs Panama, Ghana vs Croatia.

Q1.question: At halftime, will both teams have at least 1 shot on target?
Q1.compound_structure: P(ENG ≥1 HT SOT) × P(GHA ≥1 HT SOT)
key_evidence: Ghana had ZERO total shots in 1H vs Panama — not just 0 SOT, zero shots entirely
England scored at 12' and 42' in 1H vs Croatia — active attackers from kick-off
RULE7: Ghana at 3.97% = extreme underdog, attacks will be minimal throughout
England's high press creates transition opportunities for Ghana but England defense is far better than Panama

Q2.question: Will Ghana commit more fouls than England?
Q2.match_script_prediction: Ghana is the DEFENDING team from kickoff. Deep block, absorbing England's attacks. The defending team fouls more.

gd1_foul_data.note: GD1 is close — Ghana 9 vs England 10. But roles in GD2 are reversed.

Q2.partey_factor: Thomas Partey returns — defensive DM known for cynical fouls at the base of midfield. Elevates Ghana foul count.

Q3.question: Will both teams have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
key_evidence: Ghana had 2 total SOT vs Panama, both in 2H (0 in 1H) — baseline for 2H output
Against England (harder), Ghana's total SOT may drop to 1-2, still mostly in 2H
If England is 3-0 up, Ghana may push forward desperately and get 1-2 SOT
England near-certain to have 2H SOT regardless of match state

Q4.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
key_evidence: P(Ghana scores) = 38% (model); market BTTS Yes at ~37% confirms
England very likely to score 2+ goals (P=72.8%)
Binding constraint: Ghana scoring at all
Market Over 2.5 at -184 (~65%) → P(total≥3) ≈ 65% but BTTS is binding constraint

Q5.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
key_evidence: Martínez career 4.17 YC/game — HIGH card-giver
Awarded penalty in his only WC match so far
Ghana's deep block defending England = repeated fouls near the box = penalty risk
Thomas Partey (defensive DM) returning = more reckless fouls possible
England's attacking quality (Bellingham, Kane) creates box contact situations

Q6.question: Will Ghana have more shots on target than England in the second half?
key_evidence: RULE7: Ghana at 3.97% = extreme underdog. Do NOT compress toward 50%.
England will attack throughout — even managing a 3-0 lead, they'll have 4-6 2H SOT
Ghana 2H SOT: probably 0-2 total (GD1 vs weaker Panama: 2 total in 90 min)
For Ghana to WIN the 2H SOT battle: England must massively under-perform AND Ghana over-perform
Blowout scenario: if England 4-0 up, they sit back somewhat — but Ghana's quality cap limits their SOT
RULE15 partially applies: Semenyo suppressed as expected-loser forward in blowout

Q8.question: Will Harry Kane score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
key_evidence: Kane market -145 for SCORING ALONE = 59% implied
Score OR assist adds ~10-12% for assist-only scenarios
Kane GD1: 2 goals, 0 assists vs Croatia in a 4-2 match (50% of England goals)
1.74 SOT/90 at Bayern Munich 2024-25 (StatMuse confirmed)
England λ=2.574 = 2-3 expected goals → Kane involved in many attacks
Kane 1H SOT market: -170 (63% implied) — shows books expect high 1H involvement

Q9.question: Will Antoine Semenyo have at least 1 shot on target?
Q9.market_signal: No FanDuel/DraftKings lines found — weak MAIN_BOOK_ABSENCE signal
key_evidence: CRITICAL: Semenyo had 0 SOT vs Panama in GD1 (ESPN confirmed) despite being Man of the Match
His 2 shots in GD1 were all blocked or off-target
RULE15: Expected-losing-team forward in massive mismatch (3.97% Ghana win) = suppress SOT
RULE7: Extreme underdog — do not compress toward 50%
Against England's defense (better than Panama), shooting opportunities even fewer
Estimated P(Ghana total SOT): 2-3 in 90 min. Semenyo's share: 0-2 of those
FLOOR_0.25 applies

Q10.question: Will Ghana have 5 or more corner kicks?
key_evidence: Ghana deep block (38% possession vs Panama) = few sustained attacks = few corners
Corners come from sustained attacks reaching the byline — Ghana plays on the counter
RULE7: Ghana's corner count severely suppressed as extreme underdog
GHA-PAN corner count: not confirmed but Ghana had only 7 total shots — probably 3-4 corners
England 8 corners vs Croatia — England will dominate the corner count here
Corner reversal rule: if England sits back at 3-0, Ghana pushes and could get corners
But Ghana's quality cap limits how often they reach the byline
FLOOR_0.25 applied — blowout reversal could push Ghana forward and generate some corners

edge_ranking: Q1 0.23 — STRONGEST EDGE: Ghana 0 total 1H shots in GD1. Compound 'both HT SOT' suppressed. Crowd likely ~0.40.
Q6 0.18 — SECOND EDGE: Ghana more 2H SOT than England is highly unlikely. RULE7 unshaded. Crowd likely ~0.32.
Q7 0.86 — PUSH HIGH: Market -600 (86%) confirms model (86.6%). Crowd may anchor low (~0.78).
Q9 0.28 — THIRD EDGE: Semenyo 0 GD1 SOT + RULE15 + RULE7. Crowd overprices his club form.
Q10 0.25 — FOURTH EDGE: Ghana deep block = minimal corners. RULE7 + FLOOR_0.25.
Q8 0.68 — MODERATE YES: Kane market -145 for scoring alone; add assist-only probability.
Q4 0.30 — SLIGHT NO: Binding constraint is Ghana scoring (38%). Crowd likely ~0.34-0.37.
Q2 0.58 — SLIGHT YES: Ghana defends = fouls more. Partey returns. Near-coin-flip direction.
Q3 0.52 — NEUTRAL: Compound; England certain, Ghana uncertain (2 2H SOT in GD1).
Q5 0.45 — NEUTRAL: High-card referee + Ghana fouling to defend; near crowd estimate.

key_risks.Q1_risk: If Ghana gets a 1H SOT from a set-piece or counter-attack, compound becomes YES and we lose.
key_risks.Q3_risk: If England blows Ghana out 4-0 by 60' and Ghana loses all shape, they might actually get more 2H SOT (blowout reversal).
key_risks.Q8_risk: If England wins clinically (1-0, Kane uninvolved), the assist-chain doesn't fire.
key_risks.Q2_risk: If England presses high and fouls a lot in the midfield (similar to their 10 vs Croatia), the foul gap may be small.

gd1_key_lessons_applied.ghana_0_1h_shots: Applied to Q1 — Ghana HT SOT probability is low (~25%)
gd1_key_lessons_applied.semenyo_0_sot: Applied to Q9 — RULE15 reinforced by actual GD1 evidence
gd1_key_lessons_applied.england_11_sot: Confirms England will generate many SOT — applied to Q6 (Ghana unlikely to get more)
gd1_key_lessons_applied.kane_2_goals: Calibrates Q8 — Kane is in excellent form, will be central to England attacks
gd1_key_lessons_applied.ghana_deep_block: Applied to Q2 (defending team fouls more), Q10 (corners suppressed)

post_match.result_note: MASSIVE UPSET. England (86.55% model win / -600 market) drew 0-0 with Ghana. Outcome fell in the 9.48% draw probability bucket. No goals, no penalty, no red card.

Q1.note: Ghana 0 1H SOT confirmed. GD1 behavioral data prediction validated perfectly.

Q2.note: Ghana committed more fouls. Near tie with crowd.

Q3.note: Both teams had ≥1 2H SOT. Even outmatched Ghana got SOT in 2H. We underpriced this at 52%.

Q4.note: No goals at all. Correctly priced low.

Q5.note: No penalty or red card. We were above crowd at 0.45 and paid for it.

Q6.note: Ghana did not have more 2H SOT than England. RULE7 applied correctly.

Q7.note: England failed to win. 0-0 draw. Both us and crowd overpriced England win; crowd less so at 80%. This is the 9.48% draw scenario materializing. Model not wrong — high probability events still fail.

Q8.note: Kane no goal or assist in 0-0 draw. Crowd very close.

Q9.note: Semenyo 0 SOT. RULE15 applied correctly. Crowd overpriced club form at 45%.

Q10.note: Ghana did not get 5+ corners. RULE7 + deep block + FLOOR_0.25 applied.

key_learnings.L1: Q1 +35.5: GD1 behavioral data (Ghana 0 total 1H shots) was the single best predictor in match. Crowd at 61% was massively wrong. Always anchor on GD1 individual behavioral data first.
key_learnings.L2: Q9 +15.14: RULE15 applied correctly to Semenyo. 0 SOT in match. Crowd 45% overpriced club form. RULE15 + GD1 evidence together = reliable suppression signal.
key_learnings.L3: Q3 -9.28: Both teams 2H SOT. We underpriced at 52% (crowd 66%). Even massively outmatched Ghana (3.97% win probability) got ≥1 SOT in 2H. CALIBRATION UPDATE: 'Both teams 2H SOT' base rate is ~75-80% even in extreme mismatch. Distinct from 'Both teams 1H SOT' (which correctly fails for extreme underdogs).
key_learnings.L4: Q7 -7.56: England failed to win despite 86%+ probability. The 9.48% draw materialized. Not a model failure — this is within normal variance. But note: we were MORE confident than crowd (86% vs 80%) which means we ate more loss. In future extreme Elo mismatches where draw is legitimately only ~10%, consider whether crowd's slight compression (to 80%) is actually better calibrated.
key_learnings.L5: Q5 -4.09: Penalty/red card prop. We went 45% (above crowd 37%). Result NO. Historical pattern: we keep being too high on penalty/red card. Need to review our calibration on this question type across the ledger.
key_learnings.L6: 0-0 result in England-Ghana is the biggest upset of WC2026 so far. England's Elo advantage (464.87 — largest in our series) meant nothing on the day. This has implications: extreme Elo gaps do NOT guarantee goals. The model's λ_ENG=2.574 was not realized.
